title: Failed API/Login Attempt Detection

compliance.assurance_rationale: Detects repeated failed authentication attempts against the management system to support account lockout and response workflows.
compliance.controlObjective: Implements a detective control for patterns of unsuccessful logon or auth-related audit entries consistent with NIST 800-53 AC-7 expectations for the control plane when thresholds are policy-aligned.
compliance.evidenceArtifact: Alert or report from `UC-5.13.48` with threshold, ticket ID for lockout, and runbook for credential abuse investigation.

compliance.assurance_rationale: Uses audit telemetry to flag potential malicious authentication activity against the management platform (system-level monitoring of security-relevant events).
compliance.controlObjective: Contributes to continuous monitoring of information-system security by watching authentication failure patterns in Catalyst Center logs.
compliance.evidenceArtifact: Same alert, referenced in the SOC monitoring standard operating procedure for management-plane auth failures.

dataSources: index=catalyst, sourcetype cisco:dnac:audit:logs (Catalyst Center audit; auth-related request types and failure substrings in auditDescription)
spl: index=catalyst sourcetype="cisco:dnac:audit:logs" (auditRequestType="*LOGIN*" OR auditRequestType="*AUTH*") (auditDescription="*fail*" OR auditDescription="*denied*" OR auditDescription="*error*" OR auditDescription="*invalid*") | stats count as failed_attempts by auditUserName, auditIpAddress | where failed_attempts > 3 | sort -failed_attempts
description: Detects multiple failed login or API authentication attempts against Catalyst Center, which may indicate brute-force attacks or misconfigured integrations.
value: Failed authentication attempts are a primary indicator of credential attacks. Detecting them early enables account lockout and IP blocking before a breach occurs.
implementation: Enable the `audit_logs` input in the Cisco Catalyst TA pointing to `index=catalyst`. The TA polls audit log data from the Catalyst Center Intent API `/dna/intent/api/v1/audit/logs` every 5 minutes. Key fields: `auditRequestType`, `auditDescription`, `auditUserName`, `auditTimestamp`, `auditIpAddress`.
visualization: Table (auditUserName, auditIpAddress, failed_attempts), map of source IP if you enrich, alert when threshold is exceeded in a short window (use a shorter `stats` or `bin` in production).
knownFalsePositives: Many admins can share one NAT or jump address, so a few honest typos in the Catalyst UI can look like a coordinated attack in a naïve `stats` over a long window. The substring `*error*` in `auditDescription` can match benign or informational text depending on release wording. Automation that retries bad tokens during clock skew or integration work can also stack failures without an external threat actor.
detailedImplementation:
Prerequisites
• Cisco Catalyst Add-on (7538) with `cisco:dnac:audit:logs` for Catalyst **management plane** sign-in and API authentication attempts captured in audit.
• This is **not** a substitute for ISE, AD, or MFA logs—Catalyst may only see what its UI/API layer records. Combine evidence sources in SOC runbooks.
• For true brute-force use cases, add `| bin _time span=5m` and count within each bucket before the `>3` test; a single unbounded `stats` can merge days of noise into one total.
• `docs/implementation-guide.md` and `docs/guides/catalyst-center.md`.

Step 1 — Configure data collection
• Review 50 `auditDescription` samples that match `*fail*` to confirm the strings are real auth failures, not spurious `error` text.

Step 2 — Failed attempt prototype
```spl
index=catalyst sourcetype="cisco:dnac:audit:logs" (auditRequestType="*LOGIN*" OR auditRequestType="*AUTH*") (auditDescription="*fail*" OR auditDescription="*denied*" OR auditDescription="*error*" OR auditDescription="*invalid*") | stats count as failed_attempts by auditUserName, auditIpAddress | where failed_attempts > 3 | sort -failed_attempts
```

Understanding this SPL (Catalyst control plane)
**Failed API / login (heuristic)** — Reduces the audit stream to likely authentication-related `auditRequestType` values and failure-like substrings in `auditDescription`. Tighten to explicit Cisco message codes when your build documents them, and time-bucket for AC-7 style rate-based alerting.

**Pipeline walkthrough**
• Filter auth-ish types and text patterns → per `auditUserName`+`auditIpAddress` `count` → `where` threshold → `sort` by failures.

Step 3 — Validate
• In a lab, intentionally fail login and confirm one raw event and its `auditDescription` field; adjust substrings to avoid double-counting “error” in non-auth contexts.

Step 4 — Operationalize (SOC)
• Enrich `auditIpAddress` with your RFC1918, VPN, and partner ranges; different responses for on-net vs off-net. Create allowlists for health-check and backup accounts that re-auth by design.

Step 5 — Troubleshooting
• Zero rows: Catalyst may encode failures without those keywords—`| stats count by auditRequestType, auditDescription` to rebuild filters after upgrade. Too many rows: add `bin` to `_time` and require multiple buckets above threshold, or raise the numeric `>3` in line with IdP lockout policy.
grandmaExplanation: We catch repeated failed login attempts to Catalyst Center, which could mean someone is trying to break in or a system has the wrong password.
